_about: Reference targets for ddX-enabled PCM validation. PRODUCTION PCM PHYSICS IS FORTRAN-SIDE; these values are comparison targets only and never feed the SCF. See docs/solvent_pcm_literature_benchmarks.md. Two categories: 'literature_value' (transcribed from a paper with exact protocol) and 'trusted_reference_regression' (generated by ddX/pyddx or another trusted code with the exact same protocol). A row is a real pass/fail gate only when reference_value is non-null and status is 'verified'; 'pending_reference' rows are skipped even when ddX is available.

trusted_reference_regressions.description: ddPCM electrostatic solvation energy of a fixed 12-site point-charge model (no QM SCF). Validates the OpenQP<->ddX adapter numerics (model build -> solve -> esolv) only.
trusted_reference_regressions.source: source/solvent_ddx_adapter.c::oqp_ddx_run_point_charge_smoke (value asserted in-source within 1e-6 at line ~181); printed as 'energy=<float>' by tests/ddx_adapter_smoke.c

empirical_verification.note: Verified via real ddX build through oqp_ddx_adapter_smoke; the ~8e-7 difference from the pinned value is ddX-version sensitivity, still inside 1e-6. FD check: |dE/dphi0-(-0.5 q_cav0)|~9.5e-11 vs |dE/dphi0-q_cav0|~9.7e-5, confirming chg=-0.5*q_cav.

trusted_reference_regressions.validates: ddX model allocation, electrostatics, forward/adjoint solve, and esolv numerics through the OpenQP-owned C adapter
trusted_reference_regressions.does_not_validate: QM SCF coupling -- electronic contribution to psi, q_cav sign/scale into the Fock operator, phi_cav-from-density convention, and e_pcm vs 0.5*Tr[D.V] bookkeeping all remain open and require the Tier-2 QM benchmarks below

benchmarks.reference_source: pending: no exact-protocol H2O ddPCM literature_value could be transcribed (see literature_search); generate a trusted_reference_regression via pyddx with identical geometry/radii/discretization once ddX is built

literature_search.methodological_paper: Nottoli, Stamm, Scalmani, Lipparini, 'Quantum Calculations in Solution of Energies, Structures, and Properties with a Domain Decomposition Polarizable Continuum Model', J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2019, 15(11), 6061-6073
literature_search.outcome: paper located and confirmed as the correct QM ddPCM methodological reference, but no per-molecule electrostatic solvation energy with a protocol matching this path (6-31G*, Bondi-by-Z radii, lmax=8/nleb=302/eta=0.1) could be transcribed/verified (paywalled; protocol mismatch). Not used as a literature_value to avoid an unverified number.

benchmarks.doi_or_url: 10.1021/acs.jctc.9b00640 (methodological reference only; not a transcribed value)

empirical_ddx_run.vacuum_pcm_off_status: PASS (reproduces established RHF/6-31G* value to 7 places via real OpenQP build)
empirical_ddx_run.pcm_on_committed_defaults: BLOCKED: ddX ddPCM Jacobi solver does not converge (FMM on); maxiter 100->2000 does not help

pcm_on_exploratory_fmm_off_unvalidated.note: Exploratory only (FMM disabled, NOT committed). Numbers are physically wrong and are NOT a reference; recorded to expose the open conventions.
pcm_on_exploratory_fmm_off_unvalidated.exposes: e_pcm vs 0.5*Tr[D.V] differ by ~2x AND overall scale is grossly wrong; phi_cav all-positive/large; q_cav_sum ~ total nuclear charge

benchmarks.reference_source: pending: DFT smoke validates the same Fortran/C ddX PCM source/Fock diagnostics; do not use as a literature benchmark until an identical-protocol DFT reference is generated
pending: DFT smoke validates the same Fortran/C ddX PCM source/Fock diagnostics; do not use as a literature benchmark until an identical-protocol DFT reference is generated
